titleStmt.title: SII 15.114: original edition by P. B. Desai, Dines Chandra Sircar and G. S. Gai
No. 114. (B.K. No. 203 of 1926-27.) NIḌGUNDI, RON TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a stone set up inside the Basavaṇṇa temple.

This is dated the 4th regnal year of an unspecified king, Vikṛita, Śrāvaṇa-puṇṇami, Wednesday. On palaeographical grounds, the record may be assigned to the 12th century when, in the said cyclic year corresponding to 1170 A.D., the details of the date correspond to July 29. The date falls in the reign of Rāyamurāri-Sōvidēva whose 4th regnal year was Vikṛita.

It records a gift of money for purchasing corn for offerings, etc., in favour of the god Kēśava by Sēnabōva Goyvaṇṇa, son of Bammadēvanāyaka, the Ūroḍeya of Kuḍūr, who had the deity consecrated. The gift was entrusted into the hands of the Four Hundred.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.note: Engraved in continuation of No. 117 of South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XI, PT 1.
div.p:
svasti śrīmatu 4 neya vikrita saṁvatsarada śrāvaṇada puṇnami budha
vāradaṁdu nālnū¨rbba¨rgge kuḍūroḍeya baṁmadēvanāyakara maga sēnabōva gōyvaṁṇa kēśa
vādityadēvaroḷage tānu māḍisida kēśavadēva¨rgge nivēdyakkaṁ pavitrārōpaṇa maṁ naḍasuvantāgi pādapū
jeyūgi koṭṭa gadyāṇa mūvatta davasavaḷ�nellu kuḷa voṁdu mēlubīya tiṁgaḷiṁ ge vīsavaydu pavitrārōpaṇakke
paṇa pārikhāyadalu naḍasuttaṁ bapparu yi dhammamaṁ nālnū¨rvvaruṁ Ā caṁdrā¨rkatāraṁbareṁ pratipāḷisi na
ḍasuvaru .
Digital edition of SII 15.114 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
